title: Minimal high-signal strategy stack
summary: Combine only the most stable strategy mechanisms into a compact first-pass feature stack.
rationale: The run suggests the strongest reusable signal is a small set: compound-scaled tyre pressure, current-tyre finish feasibility, next-stop fresh-slick feasibility, slick-option richness, race phase, and dry-compound state. Larger stacks with many peer and rolling features did not consistently dominate.
implementation_hint: Build a single compact `preprocess` block with original columns plus race-length estimation, compound expected-life pressure, current and next-stop finish windows, fresh-slick option count/margins, current-counted slick diversity, and a few robust degradation/lap-time transforms. Exclude broad rolling and neighbor blocks in this first test.
expected_effect: Medium ROC AUC and stability gain with high confidence.
risk: May miss incremental gains from field context, but it should provide a cleaner baseline for later ablations.
